title: BOB_SCAN_SECS=120 uses wrong decryptor in scan_to_tip timeout
description: In testnet_shielded_payment_end_to_end (e2e_testnet.rs lines 489-493), the tokio::time::timeout wraps bob_scanner.scan_to_tip() but the return value (blocks_scanned count) is discarded into .await.expect() without being saved. If the scan times out, the timeout error surfaces but there is no diagnostic showing how many blocks were scanned before the deadline. Additionally, BOB_SCAN_SECS=120 is measured against SaplingIvkDecryptor (full note trial-decryption) whereas the scan_advances_tip test that informed this budget used NullDecryptor (no decryption work). IVK decryption per block is non-trivial; 120s may be adequate for a 2-3 block scan but is unvalidated for pathological cases where broadcast_height is many blocks behind.
close_reason: P3/P4 deferred: below stopping threshold (P0=0, P1=0, SEC7 says STOP)
